title: HTML Pretty Print
shortDescription: Pretty print HTML with stable spacing for reviews, audits and debugging.
metaTitle: HTML Pretty Print | Format HTML Online
metaDescription: Pretty print HTML with stable spacing for reviews, audits and debugging. Clean up markup in your browser with a fast, client-side HTML formatter.
intro: Format messy markup into readable HTML with consistent indentation and spacing. This client-side HTML pretty printer helps you review code, compare changes, and debug structure without sending data to a server.
overview: HTML Pretty Print turns compact or messy markup into clean, indented HTML that is easier to read and review. It is useful for debugging nested elements, checking attribute placement, and preparing code for audits or handoff. Processing happens in the browser for a fast, private workflow.
howItWorks: Paste your HTML into the input field.
Choose formatting options such as indentation or comment handling if available.
Generate the pretty printed output instantly in your browser.
Copy the formatted HTML and use it for reviews, debugging, or documentation.
useCases: Debugging deeply nested or minified markup.
Preparing code snippets for code reviews and audits.
Making copied HTML easier to read in documentation or tickets.

examples.title: Compact markup
examples.description: Transforms compact HTML into a readable block layout.

examples.title: Nested content
examples.description: Makes nested lists easier to inspect.

faq.question: Is this HTML pretty printer safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for client-side use and should handle HTML safely with XSS-aware processing.

faq.question: Does formatting change the content?
faq.answer: The tool focuses on whitespace and indentation. It should preserve the meaning of your HTML while making it easier to read.

faq.question: Can I use it on minified HTML?
faq.answer: Yes. It is ideal for minified or compact markup that needs to be expanded for review or debugging.

faq.question: Does it work in the browser only?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is client-side only and does not require external APIs.
